title: Cake And Sack
content:
Old King Caraway
        Supped on cake,
    And a cup of sack
        His thirst to slake;
    Bird in arras
        And hound in hall
    Watched very softly
        Or not at all;
    Fire in the middle,
        Stone all round
    Changed not, heeded not,
        Made no sound;
    All by himself
        At the Table High
    He'd nibble and sip
        While his dreams slipped by;
    And when he had finished,
        He'd nod and say,
    'Cake and sack
        For King Caraway!'
meaning:
"Cake And Sack" is a quintessential example of Walter De La Mare's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
